items.title: PM stands by decision not to join strikes on Iran and sends more jets to Qatar
items.snippet: The PM said the best way forward was a "negotiated settlement with Iran where they give up their nuclear ambitions".

items.title: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
items.snippet: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azimi.

items.title: Iran school and nearby military base struck multiple times, satellite image reveals
items.snippet: Iranian authorities say the attack, which took place on Saturday, killed 168 people.

items.title: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
items.snippet: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

items.title: Labour MP 'voluntarily suspends herself' after husband arrested in China spy probe
items.snippet: Joani Reid said she had temporarily stood down from the party after her husband was arrested on suspicion of spying for China.

items.title: Covid inquiry chair defends £200m cost and four-year process on final day
items.snippet: Baroness Heather Hallett said completing the hearings in under four years was an achievement but critics have questioned its cost.
